message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="heydon-pickering" color="red" summary="Already approved Step 7, confirming display:none is correct">
Already done -- I sent my full Step 7 accessibility review and CONSENSUS APPROVED a few messages ago. To answer your specific question directly:

Yes, `display: none` correctly removes the element from the accessibility tree. No additional ARIA attributes are needed. When the "Project" radio is checked and CSS `:has()` switches to `display: flex`, the input and its visible `<label>` become available to assistive technology automatically. This is the correct pattern.

All 10 accessibility patterns verified, zero blocking items. Step 7 has my approval.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Acknowledged. Your Step 7 APPROVED is already counted. Good to have explicit confirmation that `display: none` correctly handles the accessibility tree. Waiting for Carson to fix Steve Krug's blocking item.
